title: Frangipane mince pies
subtitle: Sweet shortcrust mince pies with an almond frangipane topping
excerpt: Mince pies made with a sweet shortcrust pastry case filled with mincemeat and topped with almond frangipane instead of a pastry lid. The frangipane bakes to a soft, macaroon-like layer over the spiced fruit. Makes twenty-four.
sourceNotes: Mince pies are one of the oldest documented British pastries, recorded with spiced dried fruit and meat fillings from the medieval period, and without meat from the early nineteenth century. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: mince pies made with a sweet pastry and a dried-fruit mincemeat are documented as a standard British Christmas preparation. The frangipane topping version appears in twentieth-century British domestic baking as a variation on the traditional closed mince pie, substituting an almond sponge for the pastry lid.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze baked and cooled mince pies in a tin for up to 1 month. Warm from frozen in a low oven for 10 minutes.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The pastry can be made and refrigerated up to 2 days ahead. The frangipane can be made a day ahead and kept refrigerated.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; the frangipane topping should be set and pale gold; 20–22 minutes

content.text: The frangipane should be level with the top of the pastry case before baking; it puffs only slightly. Do not overfill with mincemeat or the frangipane will spill over during baking. A 7 cm round cutter is the right size for a 12-hole shallow bun tin; adjust to fit your tin depth.

Rub the cold {{unsalted-butter}} (pastry portion) into the {{plain-flour}}, {{icing-sugar}} (pastry portion), and {{sea-salt-fine}} until the mixture resembles fine crumbs. Add the egg yolk and 1 tablespoon of cold water; press together until the dough just coheres. Flatten, wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Beat the softened {{unsalted-butter}} (frangipane portion) and {{caster-sugar}} together until pale. Add the {{eggs}} (frangipane portion) one at a time, beating between additions. Stir in the {{almond-flour}} and {{vanilla-extract}} until combined. The frangipane should be soft enough to spoon; refrigerate if it seems too soft.

Preheat the oven to 180°C fan. Roll the pastry to 3 mm thick and cut 24 rounds. Press each round into a greased bun tin hole. Spoon about 1 teaspoon of {{mincemeat}} into each case, filling to just under halfway. Spoon or pipe a level layer of frangipane over the mincemeat to fill the case to the brim. Bake for 20 to 22 minutes until the frangipane is set and pale gold. Leave in the tin for 5 minutes, then transfer to a rack. Dust with {{icing-sugar}} (finish portion) before serving.
